title: Company to OPC Conversion Online in India – Fees, Cost & Help
metaTitle: Company to OPC Conversion Online in India – Fees, Cost & Help
metaDescription: Convert private limited to OPC with expert support. Know company to OPC conversion process, documents required, fees, cost India & online consultant service guidance.

hero.headline: Company to OPC Conversion in India – Online Process, Fees & Cost
hero.subtext: Ideal for single-owner businesses. Professionals Law handles full legal and ROC compliance.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: The conversion of Private Company into One Person Company (OPC) is regulated by the Companies Act, 2013 read with Companies (Incorporation) Rules, 2014. This transformation enables a private company with a sole member to exist as a distinct legal entity with the simplified OPC regime. The primary objective being to provide more management capabilities to the single entrepreneur, while reaping the advantages of limited liability and corporate form. Professionals Law guides the business owners in complying 100% with all ROC requirements in the course of conversion. We do end to end compliances which includes shareholders’ approval, alteration of AOA/MOA, MCA filings to ensure hassle free transition.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: When there is only a single director and shareholder and the company is owned completely by one individual, transforming the private limited company to an OPC is advantageous. This transformation streamlines adherence and lowers administrative overhead and decision-making effectiveness. It is great for entrepreneurs who desire limited liability and complete control. Under MCA guidelines, this change may be undertaken voluntarily subject to certain conditions. With Professionals Law, we make sure that the legal process is perfect and correct documentation and quick filing helps you to concentrate on your business thus taking care of the legal part of it.

body.title: Sole Ownership with Limited Liability
body.description: OPC structure enables full control of the business while protecting personal assets from business liabilities.

body.title: Lower Compliance Burden
body.description: Compared to private companies, OPCs enjoy exemptions from certain board meetings and filing requirements.

body.title: Improved Flexibility
body.description: Decision-making is faster and more efficient as no board or shareholder approvals are required.

body.title: Perpetual Succession
body.description: OPC continues to exist regardless of changes in ownership, ensuring long-term operational continuity.

body.title: Ease of Management
body.description: With fewer formalities and a single member, day-to-day operations are streamlined and more manageable.

criteria: The private company must have only one shareholder.
The shareholder must be a natural person, Indian citizen and resident in India.
The company must have filed all its financial statements and returns with the Registrar of Companies.
The net paid-up share capital should not exceed ₹50 lakhs and annual turnover should not exceed ₹2 crores in the previous financial year.
No outstanding loans or secured creditors without prior approval of concerned parties.

documents: MOA and AOA (to be altered for OPC format)
PAN, Aadhaar and address proof of sole member/director

body.description: Pass a resolution approving conversion into OPC.
Draft new Memorandum and Articles as per OPC norms.
Submit Form INC-6 with MCA, attaching all necessary documents.
Registrar of Companies verifies the application and documentation.
Upon approval, ROC issues a new Certificate of Incorporation indicating OPC status.
Update bank, PAN, registrations and licenses accordingly.
Late filing of Form INC-6 may attract penalties of ₹100 per day of default.
Non-disclosure of facts or furnishing false information may lead to rejection and penal action under Companies Act.
Operating as an OPC without proper approval may attract fines and disqualification of director.
Failure to update statutory records post-conversion may result in compliance notices and additional fines.

body.title: Resolution & MOA/AOA Preparation
body.description: 2 to 3 Working Days

body.title: Filing of Form INC-6
body.description: 1 to 2 Working Days

body.title: Approval from ROC
body.description: 7 to 10 Working Days

body.title: Post-conversion updates
body.description: 3 to 5 Working Days

body.title: Total Time Required
body.description: 10 to 15 Working Days

body.question: Can any private limited company convert into an OPC?
body.answer: No. Only private companies with a single shareholder who is an Indian resident can convert into OPC.

body.question: Is there a minimum capital requirement for conversion?
body.answer: There is no minimum capital, but the existing capital should not exceed ₹50 lakhs.

body.question: Can a foreign national be the sole member of an OPC?
body.answer: No. Only an Indian citizen and resident can be the sole member.

body.question: Is it mandatory to appoint a nominee in OPC?
body.answer: Yes, a nominee must be appointed at the time of conversion.

body.question: Can an OPC be reconverted into a private limited company?
body.answer: Yes, conversion back is allowed under specified conditions.

body.question: Will my tax status change after conversion?
body.answer: No, OPCs are taxed as private companies under Income Tax Act.

body.question: Do I need to notify other authorities after conversion?
body.answer: Yes, PAN, GST, bank accounts and other business registrations must be updated.

body.question: What is the role of Professionals Law in the conversion?
body.answer: Professionals Law handles the legal documentation, filings and end-to-end compliance.

body.question: Can Professionals Law assist with post-conversion compliance?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law provides ongoing compliance, advisory and legal support services after conversion.
